name: GH_Archive
summary: This is the base archive class which takes care of all (de)serialization and messaging.

constructors.name: GH_Archive
constructors.summary: Initializes a new instance of the GH_Archive class

methods.name: AddMessage(GH_Message)
methods.summary: Add a new message to the record.

params.name: message
params.doc: Message to add

throws.when: Thrown if the message is a null reference.

methods.name: AddMessage(String, GH_Message_Type)
methods.signature: public void AddMessage(string messageText, GH_Message_Type messageType)
methods.summary: Add a new message to the record.

params.name: messageText
params.doc: Message text.

params.name: messageType
params.doc: Message type.

throws.when: Thrown if message text is null or empty.

methods.name: AppendObject
methods.signature: public bool AppendObject(GH_ISerializable target, string targetName)
methods.summary: Appends a target object into the root node of this archive tree. If the root doesn't exist yet, it will be created. Existing objects at the root scope will not be affected.

params.name: target
params.doc: Object to append. Cannot be null.

params.name: targetName
params.doc: Name of object to append, name must be unique.

returns.doc: True on succes, false on failure.

throws.when: Thrown if target is a null reference.
Thrown if the target_name is null or empty.

methods.name: ClearMessages
methods.summary: Remove all messages from the log.

methods.name: CreateNewRoot
methods.summary: Discards the current data tree and instantiates a new root node. This root node contains some comments, a version value containing the current version of GH_IO.dll and a DateTime value containing the current date and time.

params.name: forWriting
params.doc: If true, all data fields are reset.

methods.name: CreateTopLevelNode
methods.summary: Creates and returns a new root node for this archive in the form of a GH_IWriter instance. Typically you do not call this method. If you want to add an object to the archive, use AppendObject() instead.

params.name: rootName
params.doc: Name of root object.

returns.doc: The GH_IWriter incarnation of the GH_Chunk that represents the new Node.

params.name: data
params.doc: Byte array to deserialize.

returns.doc: True on success, false on failure.

throws.when: Thrown if data is a null reference.

params.name: xmlContent
params.doc: Xml to deserialize.

returns.doc: True on success, false on failure.

throws.when: Thrown if xml_content is not a valid Grasshopper Xml stream.

methods.name: ExtractObject
methods.signature: public bool ExtractObject(GH_ISerializable target, string targetName)
methods.summary: Extract a target object from the data tree.

params.name: target
params.doc: Object to extract. Cannot be null.

params.name: targetName
params.doc: Name of object to extract, name must identify an existing chunk.

returns.doc: True on succes, false on failure.

throws.when: Thrown if the Root node hasn't been instantiated yet.
Thrown if target is a null reference.
Thrown if the target_name is null or empty.
Thrown if the target_name cannot be resolved.

methods.name: MessageCount()
methods.summary: Gets the number of messages recorded since the most recent IO operation began.

methods.name: MessageCount(Boolean, Boolean, Boolean)
methods.signature: public int MessageCount(bool includeInfo, bool includeWarnings, bool includeErrors)
methods.summary: Gets the number of messages recorded since the most recent IO operation began. Message count can be filtered by message type.

params.name: includeInfo
params.doc: If true, info messages will be included in the count.

params.name: includeWarnings
params.doc: If true, warnings will be included in the count.

params.name: includeErrors
params.doc: If true, errors will be included in the count.

returns.doc: The number of recoded messages that pass the type filters.

methods.name: OpenFileDialog
methods.signature: public static bool OpenFileDialog(string title, ref string filePath, List<string> additionalFilters)
methods.summary: Displays a standard OpenFileDialog with all the fields set to cater for Grasshopper files.

params.name: title
params.doc: Title of the dialog. If null, the default title will be used.

params.name: filePath
params.doc: Path to file picked by user. If file_path represents a valid string, the dialog is set up to match.

params.name: additionalFilters
params.doc: A list of additional file format filters or null. Filter strings must be in typical FileDialog.Filter syntax: "File Name (*.ext)|*.ext"

returns.doc: True is user picked a path, False if user cancelled.

methods.name: ReadFromFile
methods.summary: Reads a new archive tree from a file.

params.name: fileName
params.doc: Path of file to parse.

returns.doc: True on success, false on failure. If the read operation fails, all the member fields of this archive ought to be treated as invalid.

throws.when: Thrown if the file doesn't exist.
Thrown if the file_name doesn't represent a known file extension.

methods.name: SaveFileDialog
methods.signature: public static bool SaveFileDialog(string title, ref string filePath, List<string> additionalFilters)
methods.summary: Displays a standard SaveFileDialog with all the fields set to cater for Grasshopper files.

params.name: title
params.doc: Title of the dialog. If null, the default title will be used.

params.name: filePath
params.doc: Path to file picked by user. If file_path represents a valid string, the dialog is set up to match.

params.name: additionalFilters
params.doc: A list of additional file format filters or null. Filter strings must be in typical FileDialog.Filter syntax: "File Name (*.ext)|*.ext"

returns.doc: True is user picked a new path, False if user cancelled.

methods.name: Serialize_Binary
methods.summary: Serializes the data tree into a Binary byte array.

returns.doc: A byte array containing the Binary stream.

throws.when: Thrown if the root of the current archive hasn't been instantiated yet.

methods.name: Serialize_Xml
methods.summary: Serializes the data tree into an Xml string.

returns.doc: A string containing the Xml content.

throws.when: Thrown if the root of the current archive hasn't been instantiated yet.

methods.name: ShowMessageLog
methods.summary: Displays the message log viewer. You should typically only display the viewer if the WorstCaseMessageType equals GH_Message_Type.warning or GH_Message_Type.error

returns.doc: The closing flag for the displayed log form.

methods.name: WriteToFile
methods.signature: public bool WriteToFile(string fileName, bool overwrite, bool rememberPath)
methods.summary: Writes the current tree to a file.

params.name: fileName
params.doc: Path of file to write to. If the extension is not a recognized Grasshopper extension, an exception will be thrown.

params.name: overwrite
params.doc: True to overwrite file at specified location.

params.name: rememberPath
params.doc: If True, the MRU path field will be updated to reflect the new path.

returns.doc: True on succes, false if file already exists and overwrite is set to false.

throws.when: Thrown if the root of the current archive hasn't been instantiated yet.
Thrown if file_name is either null or empty.
Thrown if the file_name doesn't represent a known file extension.

properties.name: FileName
properties.summary: Gets the filename (without extension) of the currently loaded data tree. If the path field has not been set, "unnamed" is returned.

properties.name: GetRootNode
properties.summary: Gets the root node of this archive. Typically you do not need to modify the Root. Use functions like CreateTopLevelNode(), AppendObject() and ExtractObject() instead. If you modify the Root node, you may corrupt the archive.

properties.name: GH_IO_Version
properties.summary: Gets the version number of the current GH_IO build.

properties.name: GrasshopperBinaryExtension
properties.summary: Gets the file extension (including the dot) associated with Grasshopper® Binary data.

properties.name: GrasshopperUserExtension
properties.summary: Gets the file extension (including the dot) associated with Grasshopper® User Object file.

properties.name: GrasshopperXmlExtension
properties.summary: Gets the file extension (including the dot) associated with Grasshopper® XML data.

properties.name: IsBinaryPath
properties.summary: Gets a value that indicates whether the Path field points to a Binary Grasshopper file.

properties.name: IsPath
properties.summary: Gets a value that indicates whether or not the path field has been set.

properties.name: IsXmlPath
properties.summary: Gets a value that indicates whether the Path field points to an Xml Grasshopper file.

properties.name: Messages
properties.summary: Gets the internal list of messages.

properties.name: Path
properties.summary: Gets the path to the file from which this archive was read and/or written to. If the archive hasn't been read or written yet, this field will be null.

properties.name: WorstCaseMessageType
properties.summary: Gets the worst case message type. If the record contains at least 1 error, the worst case is GH_Message_Type.error. If the record contains no errors, but at least 1 warning, the worst case is GH_Message_Type.warning. If the record contains no messages or only infos, the worst case type is GH_Message_Type.info.
